question: <QUESTION>
orig_question: List out the id number of client who choose statement of issuance after transaction are Disponent?  'POPLATEK PO OBRATU' stands for issuance after transaction
orig_masked: List out the [C14] of [C14] who choose [C4] after [C4] are [V2]?  '[V1]' stands for [C16] after [C4]; [V1] is a value of the column [C4]; [V2] is a value of the column [C16]
orig_evidence: 'POPLATEK PO OBRATU' stands for issuance after transaction
orig_gold_sql: SELECT T2.client_id FROM account AS T1 INNER JOIN disp AS T2 ON T1.account_id = T2.account_id WHERE T1.frequency = 'POPLATEK PO OBRATU' AND T2.type = 'DISPONENT'

question: <QUESTION>
orig_question: How was the build up play dribbling class for "LEI" on 2015/9/10? "LEI" refers to team_short_name = 'LEI'; on 2015/9/10 refers to  date = '2015-09-10 00:00:00'
orig_masked: How was the [C176] for "[V3]" on [V2]? "[V3]" refers to [C174] = '[V3]'; on [V2] refers to  [C189] = '[V1] 00:00:00'; [V1] is a value of the column [C189]; [V2] is a value of the column [C189]; [V3] is a value of the column [C174]
orig_evidence: "LEI" refers to team_short_name = 'LEI'; on 2015/9/10 refers to  date = '2015-09-10 00:00:00'
orig_gold_sql: SELECT t2.buildUpPlayDribblingClass FROM Team AS t1 INNER JOIN Team_Attributes AS t2 ON t1.team_api_id = t2.team_api_id WHERE t1.team_short_name = 'LEI' AND SUBSTR(t2.`date`, 1, 10) = '2015-09-10'

question: <QUESTION>
orig_question: List down the name of players who are 35 years old and above. 35 years old and above refers to datetime(CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,'localtime') - datetime(birthday) > 34
orig_masked: List down the [C126] of [T4] who are [V1] [C121] and above. [V1] [C121] and above refers to datetime(CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,'localtime') - datetime([C121]) > [V2]; [V1] is a value of the column [C121]; [V2] is a value of the column [C121]
orig_evidence: 35 years old and above refers to datetime(CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,'localtime') - datetime(birthday) > 34
orig_gold_sql: SELECT player_name FROM Player WHERE CAST((JULIANDAY('now') - JULIANDAY(birthday)) AS REAL) / 365 >= 35

question: <QUESTION>
orig_question: How many clients opened their accounts in Jesenik branch were women? A2 has region names; Woman and female share the same meaning; female refers to gender = 'F'
orig_masked: How many [T3] [T3] their [C10] in [V1] [C24] were [C12]? [C24] has [C24]; [C12] and [C12] share the same meaning; [C12] refers to [C12] = '[V2]'; [V1] is a value of the column [C24]; [V2] is a value of the column [C12]
orig_evidence: A2 has region names; Woman and female share the same meaning; female refers to gender = 'F'
orig_gold_sql: SELECT COUNT(T1.client_id) FROM client AS T1 INNER JOIN district AS T2 ON T1.district_id = T2.district_id WHERE T1.gender = 'F' AND T2.A2 = 'Jesenik'

question: <QUESTION>
orig_question: How many loan accounts are for pre-payment of duration of 24 months with weekly issuance of statement.  Frequency = 'POPLATEK TYDNE' referes to weekly statement
orig_masked: How many [T6] [T1] are for [C36] of [V2] with [C4].  [C4] = '[V1]' referes to [C4]; [V1] is a value of the column [C4]; [V2] is a value of the column [C36]
orig_evidence: Frequency = 'POPLATEK TYDNE' referes to weekly statement
orig_gold_sql: SELECT COUNT(T2.account_id) FROM account AS T1 INNER JOIN loan AS T2 ON T1.account_id = T2.account_id WHERE T2.duration = 24 AND T1.frequency = 'POPLATEK TYDNE'

question: <QUESTION>
orig_question: List out the no. of districts that have female average salary is more than 6000 but less than 10000?  A11 refers to average salary; Female mapps to gender = 'F'
orig_masked: List out the [C32] of [T5] that have [C12] [C18] is more than [V2] but less than [V1]?  [C18] refers to [C18]; [C12] mapps to [C12] = '[V3]'; [V1] is a value of the column [C18]; [V2] is a value of the column [C18]; [V3] is a value of the column [C12]
orig_evidence: A11 refers to average salary; Female mapps to gender = 'F'
orig_gold_sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT T2.district_id)  FROM client AS T1 INNER JOIN district AS T2 ON T1.district_id = T2.district_id WHERE T1.gender = 'F' AND T2.A11 BETWEEN 6000 AND 10000

question: <QUESTION>
orig_question: What is the number of committed crimes in 1995 in the district of the account with the id 532?  A15 contains information about number of committed crimes in 1995
orig_masked: What is the [C22] in the [T5] of the [T1] with the [C1] [V1]?  [C22] contains information about [C22]; [V1] is a value of the column [C1]
orig_evidence: A15 contains information about number of committed crimes in 1995
orig_gold_sql: SELECT T1.A15 FROM district AS T1 INNER JOIN `account` AS T2 ON T1.district_id = T2.district_id WHERE T2.account_id = 532

question: <QUESTION>
orig_question: What is the eye clolour of the heaviest superhero?  the heaviest superhero refers to MAX(weight_kg); eye colour refers to colour where eye_colour_id = colour.id;
orig_masked: What is the [C19] of the [C29] [T9]?  the [C29] [T9] refers to MAX([C29]); [C19] refers to [C5] where [C19] = [C6];
orig_evidence: the heaviest superhero refers to MAX(weight_kg); eye colour refers to colour where eye_colour_id = colour.id;
orig_gold_sql: SELECT T2.colour FROM superhero AS T1 INNER JOIN colour AS T2 ON T1.eye_colour_id = T2.id ORDER BY T1.weight_kg DESC LIMIT 1

question: <QUESTION>
orig_question: What percentage of players have a strength and stamina score of more than 80? strength and stamina score of more than 80 refers to stamina > 80 and strength > 80
orig_masked: What percentage of [T5] have a [C167] and [C165] [C147] of more than [V1]? [C167] and [C165] [C147] of more than [V1] refers to [C165] > [V1] and [C167] > [V1]; [V1] is a value of the column [C165]
orig_evidence: strength and stamina score of more than 80 refers to stamina > 80 and strength > 80
orig_gold_sql: SELECT CAST(COUNT(CASE WHEN strength > 80 AND stamina > 80 THEN id ELSE NULL END) AS REAL) * 100 / COUNT(id) FROM Player_Attributes t

question: <QUESTION>
orig_question: When is the birthday of the football player who has the highest overall rating? football player who has the highest overall rating refers to MAX(overall_rating);
orig_masked: When is the [C121] of the [T4] who has the highest [C153]? [T4] who has the highest [C153] refers to MAX([C153]);
orig_evidence: football player who has the highest overall rating refers to MAX(overall_rating);
orig_gold_sql: SELECT t1.birthday FROM Player AS t1 INNER JOIN Player_Attributes AS t2 ON t1.player_api_id = t2.player_api_id ORDER BY t2.overall_rating DESC LIMIT 1
